mda_name: Office of the Attorney General
as_is: Receives a lead and sends physical requests to NTSA, Lands, and BRS to find assets owned by a suspect.
Searches their local database and provides certified paper copies of ownership documents.
Prepares court affidavits manually, attaching the paper evidence collected.
Tracks the location and status of seized vehicles/properties in a local Excel sheet.
Remits funds from liquidated assets to the National Treasury via IFMIS.
to_be: Inputs a suspect's Maisha Namba into the ARA Investigation Portal.
Instantly pings BRS, NTSA, and Ardhisasa via X-Road to list all assets.
Compiles a digitally signed evidence packet and routes it to the Judiciary Case Management System.
Upon the Judge's digital signature, the system auto-locks the asset records in the source registries.
Automatically reconciles the proceeds from forfeited assets and remits them to the National Treasury via the GPA.

mda_name: Ministry of Education
process_name: Qualification Validation and Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL)
as_is: Submits application form and scans of certificates/transcripts.
Manually checks for completeness and confirms the payment of the fee.
Contacts issuing institutions (KNEC, Universities) to authenticate certificates.
Maps the qualification against the KNQF levels.
Reviews the mapping and approves the equivalency.
Manually generates the validation letter and updates the registry.
to_be: Submits a validation request via the digital portal. Does not upload certificates if records exist in registries.
Retrieves credentials automatically from national and international education registries.
Performs automated authenticity verification (checks number, institution, accreditation) and maps to KNQF.
Conducts a risk assessment to determine the approval routing (Low, Medium, or High risk).
Handles exceptions based on risk. Officer reviews medium-risk cases; the Committee reviews high-risk cases.
Generates a digitally signed validation letter, updates the central validation registry, and notifies the applicant.

mda_name: Ministry of Labour and Social Protection
as_is: A report is made regarding a child at risk. The Children Officer performs manual case registration and assigns a reference ID.
Conducts an intake and initial risk assessment to determine the immediate danger level to the child.
Carries out a detailed investigation, including home visits, witness interviews, and evidence gathering.
Holds case review meetings to assess the investigation findings and develop a formal care plan.
Executes the care plan. If necessary, processes court referrals for formal protection orders.
Places the child in an appropriate care institution or with family, executing the planned interventions.
Conducts periodic monitoring visits to ensure the child's safety and well-being are maintained.
Initiates case closure procedures once the care plan objectives have been met and the child is deemed safe.
to_be: Reports a case via a digital portal or mobile app, initiating the digital case intake process.
Performs automatic identity verification of the child and parents against national identity databases.
Runs an AI risk scoring algorithm to highlight potential emergency scenarios for the caseworker.
Uses the risk score to make an informed human decision, conducting and logging the investigation digitally.
If a court order is required, the system automates court filing directly to the judicial platform.
Receives the digital care plan and provides necessary interventions, updating the system.
Logs continuous monitoring visits into the secure digital case file.
Finalizes the intervention and executes digital case closure, archiving the record securely.

mda_name: Ministry of Information, Communications and the Digital Economy
process_name: Government ICT Project Implementation and Digital Infrastructure Management
as_is: Develops project concept and submits to ICT Authority
Reviews architecture and compliance with standards
to_be: Designs digital service aligned with GEA
Integrates with national registries via interoperability layer
Accesses service through digital government portals

mda_name: Ministry of Youth Affairs, Creative Economy and Sports
as_is: Submits application for registration along with constitution and list of officials.
Verifies the regional representation of the federation as required by the Sports Act.
Submits a grant application for international competitions or training.
Reviews booking requests and checks for conflicts with national team schedules.
Processes facility payments and grant disbursements via bank transfers.
to_be: Logs into the Sports Portal using their Maisha Namba for SSO.
Fetches the athlete's competition history and academic status via X-Road (NEMIS/KNQA).
Selects a training facility and pays the discounted "Elite Athlete" fee.
Instantly issues a digital "Access Ticket" (QR Code) and notifies the facility manager.
Automatically triggers the quarterly grant disbursement based on verified performance logs.

mda_name: Agriculture and Food Authority
as_is: Completes application, attaches paper copies of required documents, submits application, and pays the application fee.
Receives and logs the application, and reviews documentation for completeness.
May request more info if incomplete.
Verifies business registration and compliance history manually.
Schedules inspection, conducts physical premises inspection, and submits inspection report.
Reviews the officer's recommendation and makes a final approval or rejection decision.
Generates license if approved, notifies the applicant of the decision, and updates the manual registry.
to_be: Accesses eCitizen, selects the required license type, confirms auto-populated data, and makes digital payment.
Validates business registration via BRS, checks tax compliance via KRA, retrieves farmer data from KIAMIS, and auto-populates the form.
Runs risk assessment. Low-risk applications are auto-approved. Medium-risk are routed to officer review, and high-risk trigger the inspection workflow.
Handles exception-based reviews or physical inspections (schedules inspection, uploads report, reviews results).
Generates a digital license, registers it in the digital registry, notifies the applicant, and synchronizes the approval with Kentrade.

mda_name: Ministry of Information, Communications and the Digital Economy
process_name: Digital Government Infrastructure and ICT Policy Coordination
as_is: Identifies need for digital service or ICT project
Develops ICT policy, strategy, or framework
Reviews proposed ICT project for standards compliance
to_be: Designs digital service aligned with GEA
Integrates with authoritative registries via X-Road
Accesses service digitally via portal or mobile device

mda_name: Executive Office of the President
as_is: Prepares a Cabinet Memorandum and obtains approval from the Principal Secretary and Cabinet Secretary.
Receives the memorandum, logs it in the CABMEMO system, and assigns a reference.
Checks for compliance with guidelines (e.g., formatting, required attachments).
Routes the memorandum to the AG and Treasury via the system or physical dispatch for mandatory comments.
Consolidates all feedback and prepares the final agenda for the Cabinet.
to_be: Drafts and reviews the memorandum within the secure Executive Portal.
Digitally signs the final version using their National PKI (NPKI) certificate.
Routes the signed memo via X-Road to the AG and Treasury for concurrent 48-hour review.
Uses AI tools to summarize and consolidate inter-ministerial feedback for the final Cabinet brief.
Automatically creates tracking milestones in the GDMIS based on the approved Cabinet Minute.

mda_name: Executive Office of the President
as_is: Identifies a coordination need (e.g., Drought Response) and defines the deliverables required from MDAs.
Drafts a formal request letter and attaches an Excel template for data collection.
Sends the request to all relevant Principal Secretaries and Regional Commissioners via email/physical dispatch.
Follows up with MDAs via phone calls and reminders to ensure submissions are made.
Manually cleans and merges 20+ different Excel files into a single master sheet for analysis.
to_be: Defines a new national priority and sets the target KPIs in the Coordination Portal.
Maps the KPIs to existing data fields in relevant MDA registries (e.g., Health, Agriculture, Lands).
Pulls data every 24 hours via X-Road to update the national achievement scorecards.
Automatically notifies Principal Secretaries of any "at-risk" deliverables based on real data, not claims.
Focuses on resolving the "at-risk" blocks rather than manually cleaning data.

mda_name: Ministry of Education
as_is: Submits research proposal, ethical clearance, and academic certificates.
Manually checks if all documents are attached and assigns a physical file reference.
Reviews the proposal for technical validity and alignment with national priorities.
Issues a payment demand note; waits for the researcher to pay at a bank and upload the slip.
Manually verifies the bank slip against bank statements before generating the final PDF license.
to_be: Logs into the research portal. Personal and institutional data is pre-populated via IPRS and CUE.
Fetches the researcher's ethical clearance status directly from the Ethics Review Committee (ERC) portal via X-Road.
Calculates the fee and allows the researcher to pay instantly via M-Pesa or Card.
Upon successful payment, the workflow engine triggers the issuance of a digital verifiable research license.
Automatically creates a record in the National Knowledge Repository, tracking the study's start and end dates.

mda_name: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Forestry
as_is: Submits the EIA project report and pays the statutory processing fee.
Logs the submission, verifies payment, and forwards the file for screening.
Screens the project to determine if the report is sufficient or if a full EIA study is required due to high environmental impact.
For full EIAs, initiates public participation by publishing notices, collecting comments, and potentially conducting public hearings.
Conducts physical site inspection to verify details submitted in the report.
Reviews project documents and provides sector-specific comments and conditions.
Reviews the consolidated technical findings, site inspection report, and lead agency comments.
Makes the final decision: reject, approve with conditions, or approve outright. Signs the physical license.
to_be: Logs into eCitizen and submits the EIA digitally. Business ownership and expert registration are verified instantly.
Pulls the cadastral map to verify project coordinates against protected zones (e.g., riparian land).
Automatically screens the project for environmental risk and determines if a full EIA and public participation are required.
Routes the report to relevant Lead Agencies via the national service bus for a concurrent digital review.
Conducts a site inspection using a mobile device, uploading geo-tagged photos directly to the project file.
Reviews the automatically consolidated agency comments and inspection data to make a decision.
Generates a digital EIA license with a secure QR code and pushes the project coordinates to the National Environmental Dashboard.

mda_name: Ministry of Information, Communications and the Digital Economy
process_name: Digital Government Infrastructure and ICT Policy Coordination
as_is: Identifies need for digital service or ICT project
Develops ICT policy, strategy, or framework
Reviews proposed ICT project for standards compliance
to_be: Designs digital service aligned with GEA
Integrates with authoritative registries via X-Road
Accesses service digitally via portal or mobile device

mda_name: Executive Office of the President
process_name: Executive Coordination and Presidential Directives Management
as_is: Originates from Cabinet or Presidential instruction.
Analyses the directive to identify responsible MDAs and scopes required actions.
Translates the directive into formal implementation instructions and KPIs.
Dispatches instructions via memo/email to the respective Principal Secretaries.
Confirm receipt of instructions and assign internally within their MDAs.
Implement the directive tasks based on received instructions.
Compile and submit progress reports quarterly or as requested.
Consolidates received reports manually from multiple MDAs.
Performs performance assessment against original directive KPIs.
Reviews assessment to decide if implementation is satisfactory. If yes, closes directive. If no, issues corrective instructions or escalates.
to_be: Originates from Cabinet or Presidential instruction.
Analyses the directive to identify responsible MDAs and scopes required actions.
Translates the directive into formal implementation instructions and KPIs.
Dispatches instructions via memo/email to the respective Principal Secretaries.
Confirm receipt of instructions and assign internally within their MDAs.
Implement the directive tasks based on received instructions.
Compile and submit progress reports quarterly or as requested.
Consolidates received reports manually from multiple MDAs.
Performs performance assessment against original directive KPIs.
Reviews assessment to decide if implementation is satisfactory. If yes, closes directive. If no, issues corrective instructions or escalates.

mda_name: STATE DEPARTMENT FOR IMMIGRATION AND CITIZEN SERVICES
as_is: Completes online form, pays fee, attends appointment.
Captures biometrics and verifies original documents.
Delivers passports to regional offices (Mombasa, Kisumu, etc.).
to_be: **Logs into eCitizen:** Creates account or logs in using National ID Number and Password.
**Fill Passport Form:** Selects application type (First Time, Renewal, Replacement) and inputs personal/parent details.
**Upload Documents:** Uploads National ID, Birth Certificate, Passport Photo, Recommender ID, and old passport (if applicable).
**Submit Application:** Submits the digital form and generates reference number.
**Pay Passport Fee:** Pays via Mobile Money, Card, or Bank.
**Book Biometric Appointment:** Selects an Immigration Office and books available date.
**Biometric Capture:** Applicant visits Immigration Office for fingerprints, photo, and signature capture.
**Identity & Document Verification:** Verifies identity against IPRS and checks physical documents.
**Senior Officer Approval Decision:** Reviews the verified file and decides to approve or reject.
Rejections trigger a notification to the applicant.
**Queue & Print:** Approved applications enter the production queue. Passport printing takes place.
**Quality Assurance:** Printed passports undergo QA to ensure ICAO compliance and accurate chip encoding.
**Dispatch:** Passport is dispatched to the designated collection center.
**Passport Collection:** Citizen collects the printed passport in person.

mda_name: Ministry of Interior and National Administration
as_is: Reports the event at a CRS office or via the eCitizen portal.
Verifies the supporting documents (ID of parents, notification from hospital).
Manually enters the data into the CRVS system and assigns a serial entry number.
Approves the entry and signs the physical register or digital record.
Processes the payment and triggers the printing of the certificate.
to_be: Records the birth/death in the facility EMR.
Automatically verifies the parents' IDs against Maisha Namba via X-Road.
Receives the validated packet and mints a UPI (Maisha Namba) for the child.
Notifies the parents/kin via SMS/eCitizen that the registration is complete.
Accesses the verifiable digital certificate via their mobile phone for use in other government services.

mda_name: Ministry of Public Service, Gender and Affirmative Action
as_is: Logs into eCitizen and fills forms for Internship or AGPO.
Uploads PDF copies of National ID, Academic Certificates, and BRS Business Registrations.
Manually reviews the documents to ensure the applicant is between 18-35 years old.
For internships, manually matches the applicant's course of study with available slots in government agencies.
Verifies business ownership details against BRS certificates before approving the AGPO status.
to_be: Logs into eCitizen using Maisha Namba. All personal and education details are already pre-populated.
For AGPO, the system pings BRS via X-Road to verify the company's "Youth-Owned" status.
AI matching engine assigns the intern to a government agency based on the applicant's degree and the agency's needs.
Generates a digital verifiable certificate/letter with a secure QR code for instant authentication.

mda_name: Ministry of Interior and National Administration
process_name: Inmate Case Management and Rehabilitation Tracking
as_is: Escorts the inmate to the correctional facility.
Verifies the physical committal warrant from the court.
High risk of errors or forgery.
Conducts manual identity verification (checks physical ID if available, takes ink fingerprints) and assigns a unique prison number.
Creates a physical inmate case folder containing booking details and court orders.
Performs security classification (maximum/medium/minimum) and assigns a housing block.
Manages daily custody, including attendance tracking, facility transfers, and logging disciplinary issues.
Tracks rehabilitation, vocational progress, and medical appointments via periodic paper reports.
Calculates release dates manually, accounting for sentence length, time served, and remission.
Verifies release eligibility and executes physical release, parole, or handover to probation services.
to_be: Pushes a digitally signed committal warrant via X-Road to the correctional facility.
Receives the digital warrant and verifies the inmate's identity biometrically against the national registry.
Creates a comprehensive digital case profile and performs automatic security classification based on the offense and history.
Digitally tracks rehabilitation progress, behavioral scores, and vocational training achievements.
Automatically calculates sentence expiry dates in real-time, accounting for earned remission.
Triggers automated release eligibility alerts when the sentence completion date approaches.
Receives automated notifications to prepare for the inmate's reintegration.
Executes the formal digital release, updating the National Inmate Registry.

mda_name: Ministry of Information, Communications and the Digital Economy
as_is: Submits a manual request for a specific historical event or footage.
Physically searches through thousands of tapes/reels in the KBC vaults.
Verifies the copyright status of the content (KBC-owned vs. shared).
Manually calculates the digitization and licensing fee.
Pays via bank deposit and brings the slip to the KBC registry for verification.
to_be: Searches for content on the KBC eCitizen Portal using keywords (e.g., "Madaraka Day 1963").
Displays high-resolution previews and automatically calculates the license fee based on the client's profile (Commercial vs. Academic).
Authorizes an M-Pesa payment through the GPA.
Upon payment confirmation, the system triggers an "Asset Fetch" from the secure government content lake.
Generates a time-bound download link and a verifiable digital license for the content.

mda_name: Ministry of Water, Sanitation and Irrigation
as_is: Identifies the study area and collects site data for feasibility.
Reviews the feasibility report and detailed design for approval.
Manages the tender process from advertisement to contract signing.
Oversees construction, conducts site inspections, and verifies material quality.
Conducts final tests (Pressure/Flow) before issuing a certificate of completion.
to_be: Checks the proposed project against the GIS-based Water Master Plan.
Routes the feasibility documents to NEMA and Treasury for parallel digital approval.
Submits progress reports via a secure mobile app; IoT sensors verify flow/pressure levels during testing.
Digitally signs the IPC (Interim Payment Certificate) using their NPKI certificate.
Automatically reconciles the certificate with the contract value and triggers payment via the GPA.

mda_name: Ministry of Labour and Social Protection
as_is: Identifies vulnerable households within their jurisdiction and conducts outreach drives.
Captures household information and verifies supporting documents (e.g., ID cards).
Compiles preliminary beneficiary lists based on collected data.
Conducts community validation meetings to review and cross-check the preliminary list.
Cross-checks eligibility criteria manually and finalizes the beneficiary list.
Reviews the finalized list and formally endorses the approved beneficiaries for assistance.
Forwards the endorsed beneficiary list to the Payment or Logistics Unit.
Disburses cash payments or distributes in-kind food/supplies to beneficiaries.
Acknowledges receipt of the assistance via signature or thumbprint.
Records the assistance delivered and handles any grievances or appeals raised by the community.
to_be: Registers for support via the online social protection portal or with the help of a Registration Officer.
Instantly verifies the applicant's identity using biometrics against the national identity registry.
Fetches comprehensive household and socioeconomic data from the National Social Registry via X-Road.
Performs automated eligibility scoring (means testing) based on predefined programme criteria.
Reviews the system-generated recommendations and provides final digital approval for the beneficiary list.
Enrolls the approved beneficiary into the specific programme and updates the Beneficiary Registry.
Triggers a digital payment instruction to the Government Payment Aggregator for instant disbursement to the beneficiary's mobile wallet.
Automatically notifies the beneficiary via SMS or the eCitizen app regarding their enrollment status and payment receipt.

mda_name: MINISTRY OF HEALTH
as_is: Registration occurs and a local facility ID is created.
Performs triage and consultation, followed by lab tests and prescriptions.
Data is stored in the facility EMR.
Patient visits another facility for further care.
Facility searches local EMR for the patient's record.
No record found. A new patient ID is created at the second facility.
Duplicate clinical records created without visibility into previous history.
Results in fragmented medical history and duplicate testing.
to_be: Patient identified using Maisha Namba or biometrics.
Facility system queries Master Patient Index (MPI) to locate the patient's unified profile.
Patient profile retrieved. System retrieves Shared Health Record (SHR) upon authorization.
Clinician performs consultation with access to unified history, lab results, and medications.
Encounter recorded, including treatments and e-prescriptions.
Data shared via HIE (pushed to KHIE platform).
Updates the national Shared Health Record (SHR) immediately with new encounter data.
Claims triggered for Social Health Authority (SHA) based on the recorded encounter.

mda_name: Ministry of Energy and Petroleum
as_is: Receives mail, sorts it into priority categories (Urgent/Normal/Confidential), and date-stamps it.
Classifies the document by subject and registers it in a manual mail book.
Reviews the file, prepares a response, and routes it physically for multiple levels of approval.
Assigns a dispatch number, makes a physical copy for the file, and registers it in the dispatch book.
Periodically appraises files against the retention schedule to determine if they should be archived or disposed.
to_be: Submits an application for an energy permit via the eCitizen business portal.
Instantly pings EPRA and BRS to verify the entity's regulatory standing.
Routes the digitized file to the technical team for concurrent review (parallel workflow).
Approves the permit using a digital signature (NPKI).
Automatically archives the entire application trail in the secure government cloud with AI-generated metadata.

mda_name: Ministry of Youth Affairs, Creative Economy and Sports
as_is: Coordinates the onboarding of the cooperative and aggregation of beadwork from members. Travels to the aggregation center.
Receives products, counts items, and verifies if the cooperative is formally registered with Ushanga Kenya.
Examines craftsmanship and materials to assign a Grade (A, B, or Fail) during the quality grading process.
Stores the graded inventory securely and logs items into the local catalog.
Manages market cataloging and placement, displaying products in retail shops, exhibitions, or online portals.
Facilitates product sales and records transaction details.
Conducts sales reconciliation, calculates gross earnings, and deducts management fees.
Processes payment settlement via batch bank transfers to the respective cooperatives.
to_be: Performs digital product intake using a mobile inventory scanning app, instantly verifying the cooperative's registration.
Uses an AI-assisted tool to evaluate the product, but provides the final human approval for the quality pass.
Generates a unique QR code serving as the product's digital identity and prints the physical label.
Performs inventory synchronization and automatically pushes the catalog listing to the Creative Economy Marketplace Platform.
Browses the marketplace and completes a customer purchase using integrated digital payments.
Executes an automated payment settlement, instantly splitting revenue between the artisan, cooperative, and program management.
Sends a real-time notification to the cooperative and artisan regarding the successful sale and payment.

mda_name: Ministry of Interior and National Administration
as_is: Receives asylum seeker, verifies entry documents, and captures photos/fingerprints to issue an Asylum Pass.
Conducts the RSD interview, records testimony, and reviews evidence.
Deliberates on the case applying the 1951 Criteria.
If recognized, the refugee applies for an ID Card. Old records are retrieved and prints verified.
If requested, processes Convention Travel Documents, requiring manual status verification.
to_be: Captures biometrics via integrated kits. System instantly queries IPRS and Immigration databases via X-Road to ensure the individual is not already registered.
Auto-generates a Digital Asylum Pass with a QR code verifiable by law enforcement.
Reviews the digitized evidence file and records the decision directly in the system.
Upon approval, the system pushes the data to IPRS to mint a Refugee Maisha Namba.
ID Cards and Travel Documents are queued for printing at the centralized government facility and dispatched via Posta.

mda_name: Executive Office of the President
as_is: Receives a Presidential Directive and translates it into specific instructions for relevant Ministries.
Identify the specific MDAs responsible for various deliverables within the directive.
Dispatches instructions via official correspondence (letters/memos) to Principal Secretaries.
MDAs submit quarterly reports on the status of implementation.
Manually consolidates reports, identifies gaps, and prepares a summary for the Head of Public Service.
to_be: Enters a directive into the Executive Coordination Portal, defining the Lead MDA and key milestones.
Instantly notifies relevant Principal Secretaries and creates a tracking record in the national service bus.
Periodically fetches implementation data from authoritative registries via X-Road to validate MDA claims.
Monitor the "Executive Heatmap" dashboard to identify underperforming sectors.
Generates a weekly "Situation Report" for the President, highlighting critical paths and compliance scores.

mda_name: Ministry of Co-operatives and MSMEs
as_is: Accesses the fund portal (USSD/Web) and registers using their National ID.
Performs KYC by querying IPRS to verify identity and age eligibility.
Checks internal credit history and external CRB scores to determine the loan limit.
For Uwezo/NYOTA, attaches scanned copies of business registrations or ID documents.
For group loans, verifies group composition and bank details manually at the Sub-County level.
to_be: Logs in via eCitizen SSO and selects the appropriate MSME fund or support program.
Fetches business history from BRS and tax compliance from KRA via X-Road to assess eligibility.
For group applications, the system pings the members' phones for digital consent and verifies their "Maisha Namba" status.
Instantly disburses the loan to a digital wallet managed by the Government Payment Aggregator.
Monitors sales and transactions via the GPA to provide real-time credit score updates and nudge for repayments.
